clues: A new laptop cannot connect to the company's 6 GHz Wi-Fi network.
Other devices in the office connect to the same network without issues.
The laptop has Wi-Fi 6E capability advertised on its specifications.
The Wi-Fi adapter properties show that it only supports 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands.
The laptop's Wi-Fi driver or firmware does not support the 6 GHz band despite the hardware being capable.
